entity_name: Top 24 Electronic Manufacturing Companies in Georgia
canonical_name: Top 24 Electronic Manufacturing Companies in Georgia
facts: The electronic manufacturing industry in Georgia is a vital sector characterized by companies specializing in circuit board assembly to semiconductor fabrication, meeting demands for automotive electronics among other markets.
The sector is anticipated to grow driven by increasing consumer electronics consumption and technological integration across industries.
Investments in research and development enhance capabilities, making Georgia a hub for electronic manufacturing endeavors.
The list features 24 standout electronic manufacturing companies based in Georgia, established between 1983 and 2021.
These companies are primarily located in urban centers like Norcross, Alpharetta, and Atlanta.
They specialize in areas including PCB assembly, design engineering, and semiconductor packaging, catering to industries such as defense and telecommunications.
Engent, Inc. is headquartered in Norcross, Georgia, founded in 2002, with a headcount of 1001-5000 employees.
Engent specializes in high volume manufacturing, electronic assembly, prototyping, and engineering consulting.
Engent recently acquired rights to Siemens Dematic's advanced assembly technology to enhance manufacturing capabilities.
Prime Technological Services is headquartered in Suwanee, Georgia, founded in 1989, with a headcount of 201-500 employees.
Prime Technological Services specializes in PCB assembly and system integration, serving aerospace, medical, and telecommunications sectors.
Prime Technological Services operates four precision surface mount technology (SMT) lines and holds ISO certifications.
Printed Circuits Corp. is headquartered in Lilburn, Georgia, founded in 2000, with a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Printed Circuits Corp. operates from a 32,000 square foot facility and holds AS9100D and ISO 9001:2015 certifications.
Printed Circuits Corp. offers rapid prototyping, box builds, cable/wire harness assemblies, and reverse engineering services.
i-Tech e-Services is headquartered in Norcross, Georgia, founded in 1997, with a headcount of 11-50 employees.
i-Tech e-Services specializes in high-mix, low to mid-volume PCB assemblies supported by SMT lines equipped with MYDATA and Juki pick-and-place machines.
i-Tech e-Services holds AS9100D and ISO 13485 certifications and counts Panasonic USA and Georgia Power among its clients.
i-Tech e-Services received an award for being a valuable provider by Partnership Gwinnett in 2014.
Tier One, Inc. is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 2001, with a headcount of 1-10 employees.
Tier One, Inc. has designed over 1,000 complex printed circuit board assemblies and offers the Tier One Gateway IoT platform and GNSS modules.
PPI is headquartered in Athens, Georgia, founded in 1996, with a headcount of 51-200 employees.
PPI specializes in metal stamping, electronics manufacturing, and sustainable product development, and acquired South Dakota Partners for PCBA capabilities.
East West Manufacturing is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 2001, with a headcount of 1001-5000 employees.
East West Manufacturing secured approximately $76.9 million in funding in January 2022 and operates across the United States, Costa Rica, Canada, Mexico, and Vietnam.
Dujud is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 2019, with a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Dujud specializes in semiconductor research and additive manufacturing technologies, developing proprietary tech for the 3D printing of micro-electronics like circuit boards and antennas.
Gradient Technologies is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 2010, with a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Gradient Technologies serves consumer electronics, automotive, healthcare, and industrial automation sectors with teams in the United States, India, and Belgium.
Central One is headquartered in Fayetteville, Georgia, founded in 2010, with a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Central One holds ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001, ISO 13485, and TS 16949 certifications and serves automotive, aerospace, medical, and consumer electronics sectors.
Silicon Creations is headquartered in Lawrenceville, Georgia, founded in 2006, with a headcount of 51-200 employees (approx. 150).
Silicon Creations specializes in silicon intellectual property solutions including phase-locked loops and serializer-deserializer interfaces, with IP in production across 5nm to 180nm process nodes.
ProBrand International, Inc. is headquartered in Marietta, Georgia, founded in 1983, with a headcount of 501-1000 employees.
ProBrand International invested in a state-of-the-art LNB manufacturing facility and provides low-power IoT solutions for agriculture, healthcare, and logistics.
Flip Electronics is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 2015, with a headcount of 51-200 employees (approx. 79).
Flip Electronics partnered with Ampleon to supply legacy RF transistors and serves military aerospace, medical, and industrial OEMs.
Innovative Circuits, Inc. is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 1998, with a headcount of 51-200 employees.
Innovative Circuits, Inc. holds ISO 9001:2015 certification and is ITAR registered, specializing in high-tech multilayer rigid PCBs and flexible circuits.
ArgonFDS is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 1990, with a headcount of 11-50 employees (approx. 103).
ArgonFDS manufactures rugged displays and computing devices for military applications and is part of the Spectra Aerospace & Defense group.
Delta Sigma Company is headquartered in Kennesaw, Georgia, founded in 1990, with a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Delta Sigma Company serves aerospace and automotive sectors, supported stealth F-117 Nighthawk and F-22 Raptor programs, and partners with Fanuc Robotics.
Rydev is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 2020, with a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Rydev operates as an ASIC design house for semiconductors with teams in Costa Rica and Argentina, and offers training workshops to bridge technical gaps.
Whitepath Fab Tech is headquartered in Ellijay, Georgia, founded in 1989, with a headcount of 201-500 employees (approx. 92).
Whitepath Fab Tech specializes in electrical distribution systems, wire harnesses, and control box assemblies as part of Electrical Components International.
Absolics Inc. is headquartered in Covington, Georgia, founded in 2021, with a headcount of 51-200 employees (approx. 82).
Absolics Inc. specializes in advanced packaging and glass substrate solutions for high-performance computing, data centers, and AI, and received a $100 million grant in January 2025.
Absolics Inc. is an affiliate of SKC, part of the SK Group.
Genesis Technology USA is headquartered in Norcross, Georgia, founded in 1994, with a headcount of 501-1000 employees.
Genesis Technology USA also known as Genesis Connected Solutions specializes in high-speed connectors and cables including SFP, QSFP, SlimSAS, and USB connectors.
World Micro Inc is headquartered in Roswell, Georgia, founded in 1994, with a headcount of 11-50 employees (approx. 67).
World Micro Inc maintains an average quality rating of 99.9% and serves Defense, Aerospace, and Commercial & Industrial sectors with operations in the United States, Mexico, and Malaysia.
Naeco, LLC is headquartered in Dunwoody, Georgia, founded in 1999, with a headcount of 11-50 employees (approx. 37).
Naeco, LLC holds ISO 9001, ISO 13485, and AS9100D certifications and received a $78,906 grant in March 2024 for R&D efforts.
Flight Display Systems is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, founded in 1999, with a headcount of 51-200 employees.
Flight Display Systems also known as ArgonFDS specializes in rugged display solutions and computing systems designed for military applications across air, land, and sea platforms.
